text: Tyrese Haliburton's assist-to-turnover ratio this playoffs (4.2) is the best of any primary ball-handler in the last 10 years. The Pacers' half-court offense runs through pick-and-roll reads that most teams can't replicate without a PG of his caliber.
label: analysis

text: People forget that the '16 Warriors had the easiest path to the Finals in recent memory. Only one team they faced finished above .550. Context matters when comparing dynasties.
label: analysis

text: Offensively the Pacers rank 4th in pace and 7th in points in the paint this season. Their transition offense generates 18.3 PPG which is 2nd in the league. This is a system built around speed not star power.
label: analysis

text: Haliburton's clutch time stats this season: 31.2% usage rate (8th in the league), 52% TS in clutch situations. He is legitimately elite in the moments that matter, the data backs it up.
label: analysis

text: The Celtics' defensive rating (109.4) in the postseason is nearly 6 points better than their regular season mark. Playoff rotations and scheme adjustments account for about half that gap historically. The other half is matchup-driven.
label: analysis

text: Mitchell Robinson's offensive rebounding rate (18.7%) would rank 1st among all centers if he played enough minutes to qualify. The Knicks are leaving 4-5 extra possessions per game on the table by not playing him 30+ minutes.
label: analysis

text: OKC's young core has the highest net rating differential between regular season and first round (+8.3) of any #1 seed since the '18 Warriors. Their defensive scheme adjusts faster than any team in the league.
label: analysis

text: Historical comparison: the Pacers' current 3-point attempt rate (42% of all shots) is comparable to the '19 Rockets. Their shot distribution says championship contender even if the roster doesn't look like one on paper.
label: analysis

text: Jokic has posted a 35+ point, 15+ rebound, 10+ assist game this postseason. That triple-double threshold at those levels has happened exactly three times in playoff history before this year.
label: analysis

text: The Knicks' offensive efficiency drops 8.4 points when Brunson plays with two other guards vs. when he plays alongside two wings. The lineup construction problem is real and documented.
label: analysis

text: Boston's three-point shooting in the playoffs (38.4%) is 4 points higher than league average in the same sample. Regression is coming ? teams shooting this well over a 20-game stretch almost always see a correction in the Finals.
label: analysis

text: Luka's isolation efficiency this postseason (1.12 PPP) is in the 93rd percentile league-wide. He is being guarded by the best defenders available and still generating elite outcomes. That is not luck.
label: analysis

text: The refs gave Cleveland 4 extra free throws per game in this series relative to their season average. That's a meaningful tilt ? 4 FT attempts at 75% accuracy is 3 points per game.
label: analysis

text: SGA has the highest true shooting percentage among guards averaging 25+ points this season (63.1%). For context, that's better than Steph Curry's peak TS seasons at similar usage.
label: analysis

text: Tatum's off-ball movement creates 6.2 open looks per game that teammates convert at 41% from three. His gravity is real even when his own shot isn't falling.
label: analysis

text: The Pacers' bench unit has a +8.3 net rating in the playoffs ? best among all second units. That's sustainable because it's built on turnovers (they force 18.4 per 100 possessions with the bench lineup) not shooting variance.
label: analysis

text: When Bam Adebayo plays 35+ minutes Miami wins 71% of games. When he plays under 30 they win 43%. There is a direct causal relationship between his minutes and their outcomes that the coaching staff keeps ignoring.
label: analysis

text: Giannis averages 31.4 points in the paint per game this season, the highest of any player since Shaquille O'Neal in 2001. The comparison is uncomfortable but statistically valid.
label: analysis

text: The Suns' defensive scheme is the oldest in the league ? their drop coverage works against mid-range heavy offenses but gives up 1.18 PPP on pick-and-roll to shooters. Every team with above-average corner three shooting has exploited this.
label: analysis

text: LeBron's PER in his age-39 season (23.1) is higher than every Hall-of-Famer except Jordan at equivalent ages. The durability argument is over ? he is statistically defying the aging curve.
label: analysis

text: Curry's off-dribble three-point shooting (43.2% this season) is 8 points better than the next best shooter in the same sample. No one else in the league attempts that volume at that efficiency.
label: analysis

text: Minnesota's switch-everything defense allows 0.84 PPP on all isolation ? that is elite. Their transition defense (0.98 PPP) is where they are vulnerable. Dallas should be pushing pace on every possession.
label: analysis

text: Durant's scoring efficiency (63% TS) this postseason is higher than any player averaging 25+ points. He is the most efficient volume scorer in playoff history by this measure.
label: analysis

text: The Warriors' dynasty window from 2015-2019 produced a 68% win rate in playoff games. No other team in the three-point era has sustained that level of postseason dominance over a 5-year span.
label: analysis

text: Anthony Edwards' usage rate in the playoffs (32.4%) combined with his 58.4% TS is a historically rare combination. Players at this usage level typically post TS closer to 54-55%.
label: analysis

text: Wembanyama's block rate (7.4%) as a rookie is 1.8 standard deviations above the mean for first-year big men in the shot-blocking era. His defensive impact is not projection ? it is already elite.
label: analysis

text: The Thunder have allowed the fewest points in the paint per game (38.2) in the postseason, down from an already elite 42.1 in the regular season. Their drop coverage adjustments are working.
label: analysis

text: Doncic shoots 47% on pull-up jumpers from 15-19 feet. That mid-range zone is where defenses concede to prevent the three, and he is punishing them for it at a higher rate than any player since Dirk.
label: analysis

text: Cleveland's turnover rate (11.2%) is the lowest in the league this postseason. For a team that relies on transition scoring, protecting possession is the single biggest factor in their success.
label: analysis

text: The Celtics have won 84% of their playoff games when they make 14+ three-pointers. They are 44% from three as a team this postseason. The model is working exactly as designed.
label: analysis

text: Kawhi Leonard's load management has correlated with a 12-game playoff longevity benefit on average versus comparable wings who played full seasons. The data supports the decision even when the optics don't.
label: analysis

text: OKC's pace of play (103.2 possessions per game) is the highest in the league. At that pace they generate 8.6 extra possessions per game versus a league-average team. Each possession is worth roughly 1.1 points. That is a structural advantage.
label: analysis

text: Brunson's late-game isolation (last 5 minutes of close games): 1.14 PPP, 27 possessions this playoffs. That is All-NBA closer territory. He is not just a regular season guy.
label: analysis

text: Portland's rebuild is ahead of schedule by every metric ? their developmental pipeline has produced three players who outperformed their draft slot by at least 15 WAR-equivalent points. That front office deserves more credit.
label: analysis

text: Miami's zone defense forces opponents into 24% mid-range shot rates (league average is 17%). Mid-range shots are inefficient ? that's the point. They are trading volume for quality in a way most teams cannot sustain.
label: analysis

text: The Nuggets' three-peat probability based on historical models: 22%. Every team that has won back-to-back since 2005 has faced roster attrition via salary cap. Denver has three players entering extension years simultaneously.
label: analysis

text: Jaylen Brown's defense this postseason ? opponent field goal percentage when he is the primary defender (42.1%) is 6 points below their season average. His defensive impact is being undersold in MVP conversations.
label: analysis

text: Victor Wembanyama's PER through his first 50 games (24.8) is the highest for any center since Shaquille O'Neal's 1992-93 season. He is historically ahead of every modern big man development curve.
label: analysis

text: Trae Young's net rating on the road in playoff games (-9.2) vs. at home (+5.1) is the largest home/away differential among primary ball-handlers. The crowd noise impact on his hearing-dependent play-calling is a real and measurable factor.
label: analysis

text: The Pacers' winning formula is simple in the data: when they force 16+ turnovers they are 11-1. When they force fewer than 14 they are 2-8. Turnovers are not incidental to their system ? they are the system.
label: analysis

text: Denver's transition defense (0.92 PPP allowed on the break) is 8th in the league. When teams push pace against them they are vulnerable, which is exactly what Indiana is trying to exploit.

text: Whatever happens in this series I am just grateful to watch this team compete.
